ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for butterfly-cut grilled pork steak; closest defensible match is pork steak food product (FOODON_03302051).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers grilled pig meat broadly; no specific butterfly-cut sub-code exists. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grilled pork butterfly steak; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled pork butterfly steak is approximately 150 g, representing one butterflied pork steak piece as commonly served in a Western-style meal or hawker setting.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Pork Butterfly Steak — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Grilled Pork Butterfly Steak (cr_id F06090008). All core macros and micronutrients are taken from this exact record. Saturated fat data not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork loin, broiled (NDB No. 10062) as the closest approved-source analogue, documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate food; reference: University of Sydney GI Database methodology for zero-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled pork butterfly steak contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Per University of Sydney GI Database methodology, glycemic index testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load of 25–50 g; foods with negligible carbohydrate content are not assigned a GI value. gi_category is set to not_applicable. glycemic_index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel indicating no glycaemic response (not a measured GI value). Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (0 × 0.0) / 100 = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (45 mg/100 g) and high potassium (427 mg/100 g) yield a favourable Na:K ratio consistent with DASH dietary targets.
hypertension.guidance: Grilled pork butterfly steak is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt or high-sodium marinades; its naturally low sodium and high potassium content support blood pressure management per DASH guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar eliminate glycaemic impact; high protein (30.6 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean mass preservation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled pork butterfly steak produces no glycaemic response and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when served without sugary marinades, glazes, or sauces, in line with ADA dietary recommendations for carbohydrate minimisation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.9 g/100 g) and cholesterol (123 mg/100 g) require portion awareness; lean muscle content and low sodium are positive factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may include grilled pork butterfly steak in moderate portions (≤150 g), choosing lean cuts and avoiding added fats, consistent with AHA guidance to limit saturated fat to less than 6% of total daily energy intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contains arachidonic acid (omega-6) which may amplify pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways relevant to RA; trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) is additionally pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including pork and consider substituting with oily fish rich in omega-3 fatty acids; if consuming, pair with anti-inflammatory vegetables and avoid high-sodium condiments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled pork butterfly steak is a Western-style preparation of boneless pork — typically from the loin or shoulder — that has been butterflied (sliced open and flattened to an even thickness) before being grilled over direct heat until cooked through. The butterfly cut maximises surface area, producing a slightly charred, caramelised exterior while retaining a juicy, tender interior. The cut retains both lean muscle meat and attached internal and external fat, contributing to its characteristic flavour and mouthfeel. Per 100 g, it delivers 197 kcal, 30.6 g protein, 8.2 g fat, and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for ketogenic, low-carbohydrate, and high-protein dietary patterns. Sodium is very low at 45 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 427 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary guidelines. Cholesterol is 123 mg/100 g, which warrants moderate portion awareness for individuals with cardiovascular disease. The food is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is commonly served as a main protein at breakfast or dinner in Singapore hawker centres and Western-style restaurants. Clinically, it is well-suited for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its zero glycaemic impact, and for hypertension management due to its low sodium and high potassium profile. It is not recommended as a primary protein for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its omega-6 arachidonic acid content.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06090008, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork loin broiled entry as the closest approved-source analogue and documented accordingly.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06090008); this food is a plain grilled pork product with no carbohydrate-containing ingredients — naturally zero for a pure animal-muscle product. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per the same source — naturally zero. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a pure animal-muscle product — naturally zero. Saturated_fat_g (2.9 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.6 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.9 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 10062 (Pork, loin, whole, separable lean and fat, cooked, broiled) as the closest approved-source analogue; HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry. Trans_fat_g estimated at 0.1 g/100 g from USDA SR Legacy pork loin broiled data; documented as trace. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this specific food item (F06090008) and were not supplemented from USDA SR Legacy in this iteration to avoid over-attribution; follow-up enrichment from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork loin broiled is recommended. Glycemic index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel value indicating no glycaemic response; gi_category is set to not_applicable because this food contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and GI testing is not applicable per University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation. This is a Western-cuisine food item; no Singaporean-dish multilingual naming requirements apply (G6 Singaporean rule does not trigger). Halal status is not confirmed as pork is not halal by definition.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Grilled Pork Butterfly Steak (F06090008) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The sole failure from iteration 2 (q4: glycemic_index must be numeric) has been resolved by setting glycemic_index to 0 as a numeric sentinel value, with gi_category remaining not_applicable and full gi_source documentation explaining the not-applicable rationale per University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA SR Legacy pork loin broiled and documented. Zero carbohydrate, sugar, and fibre values are confirmed and justified as naturally zero for plain grilled pork. Ontology anchors are defensible approximations with honest gap notes. All three mandatory health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance including Type 2 Diabetes with correct human_condition_name. Aliases are comprehensive and internally consistent. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context. Western cuisine — no non-English multilingual naming requirements apply.
